guide.title: Guide & help
guide.subtitle: Statuses, calculations & covers
guide.statusesIntro: Every volume carries a status. It drives your counters and the value of your collection.

owned.name: Owned
owned.desc: You have this volume in your library. It counts toward “owned volumes” and your collection value.

read.name: Read
read.desc: You finished this volume. Only available on an owned volume; feeds your reading progress.

wished.name: Wished
wished.desc: A volume you want to buy. It joins your wishlist and the wished value.

announced.name: Announced
announced.desc: An upcoming volume, not yet on sale. Handy to track future releases.

untracked.name: Untracked
untracked.desc: No status: the volume exists in the series but counts toward nothing.

guide.rule1: Marking a volume “Owned” automatically removes it from the wishlist and announced lists.
guide.rule2: “Read” is only offered on an owned volume.
guide.rule3: “Wished” and “Announced” are only offered on a volume you don’t own.
guide.rule4: The series reading status (In progress, Completed…) updates itself based on owned and read volumes.
guide.dashboardIntro: The dashboard aggregates all your volumes in real time. Here is how each number is computed.

series.name: Series
series.formula: The number of works in your collection.

owned.name: Owned volumes
owned.formula: The total of volumes marked “Owned” across all series.

progress.name: Reading progress
progress.formula: Read volumes ÷ owned volumes, as a percentage.

wishlist.name: Wishlist
wishlist.formula: The “Wished” volumes you do not own yet.

ownedValue.name: Owned value
ownedValue.formula: The sum of the prices of all owned volumes.

wishlistValue.name: Wished value
wishlistValue.formula: The sum of the prices of wished, not-owned volumes.

genre.name: Genre breakdown
genre.formula: The number of series grouped by genre.

guide.priceNote: Values rely on each volume’s price. Set it per volume, or apply one price to the whole series at once with “Unit price (bulk)”. A volume with no price counts as €0.
guide.coversIntro: Several ways to find the right cover for a volume. Pick whichever suits you.

isbnExplainer.title: What is an ISBN?
isbnExplainer.line1: It’s a book’s unique number. It comes in two lengths: 10 digits (older books) or 13 digits. Ziggythèque accepts both.
isbnExplainer.line2: You’ll find it on the back of the book, just above or below the barcode.
isbnExplainer.barcodeLabel: barcode on the back of the book

search.name: Search by title
search.desc: The fastest method for manga. We search by title + volume number and show cover suggestions.
search.step1: The field is pre-filled with the title and volume — tweak it if needed.
search.step2: Pick the source via the logo on the left: Auto (smart cascade), MangaDex or Google Books.
search.step3: Click a thumbnail to apply the cover to the volume.

isbn.name: Search by ISBN
isbn.desc: The ISBN is the 10 or 13 digits on the back of the book, near the barcode. The most accurate method.
isbn.step1: Enter the ISBN (10 or 13 digits) then run the search.
isbn.step2: We query several databases and group the results by source.
isbn.step3: Click the cover you prefer to apply it.

scan.name: Barcode scan
scan.desc: Scan the barcode to grab the ISBN automatically, without typing anything.
scan.step1: Computer camera: allow the camera and show the barcode.
scan.step2: Or “Scan with my phone”: a QR code appears, open it on your phone.
scan.step3: The barcode read on the phone is sent here instantly and the search starts.

url.name: Paste a URL
url.desc: Already have the image? Paste its URL at the bottom of the window and apply it directly.

auto.name: Auto-complete
auto.desc: From the series page, the “Complete covers” button fills all missing volume covers at once.

guide.coverSourcesNote: Title search: MangaDex and Google Books. ISBN search: all sources above, grouped by provider.
